reasoning: Peer helmets exhibit classic Denzi chunky 3D-suggestive pixel art: multi-pass shading, bright specular highlights on upper faces, clear top-left light source with bottom-right cast shadows, and expressive 'alive' character. The key_iron_v01 candidate breaks on 4 critical dims: (1) Highlight intensity — the key is nearly flat with no visible specular pop, while every peer helmet has a bright highlight chip; (2) Shadow count — peers show at least two shadow zones (core shadow + drop shadow or cast shadow), the key shows a single flat shading pass at most; (3) Detail density — at comparable pixel scale the peers pack in rivets, visor slits, plume overlaps, cheek-plate edges; the key is a minimal silhouette read with almost no internal pixel articulation; (4) Rendering style — helmets read as solid 3D objects; key reads as a flat 2D icon, closer to a UI glyph than a world-drop sprite. Charm level also fails: the peer family has personality and weight; the key feels like placeholder clip-art. Outline weight and color are close enough (dark 1px perimeter), and color temperature shares a cool-metallic range, so those two dims partially save the score from 1-2 territory. Net: 4/10, fail.
